feature: Catalog signal routing to app-development specialist
user_story: As a Qlik user with an app-development issue, I want matching request text to select the app-dev specialist so the agent loads the narrowest useful skill.
expected_behaviour_based_on_code: Signals such as set analysis, qvd, komment, and app-development capabilities return primary_skill qlik-sense-app-dev with catalog lookup, evidence hints, tools, risk, candidates, and availability.

feature: Catalog signal routing to diagnostic specialist
user_story: As a Qlik administrator with reload or QSEoW evidence, I want diagnostic prompts to route to the diagnostic tool with app-dev fallback so operational issues use the right workflow.
expected_behaviour_based_on_code: Signals such as reload failed, qseow, and reload task return primary_skill qlik-sense-diagnostic-tool, secondary_skills containing qlik-sense-app-dev, diagnostic risk, and evidence hints.

feature: Default route for unclear Qlik requests
user_story: As an agent handling a vague Qlik request, I want a stable default to app development so the router still returns a usable specialist.
expected_behaviour_based_on_code: If no catalog signal matches, route_request defaults to qlik-sense-app-dev and explains that no specific signal matched.

user_story: As a user referring to a skill by alias or capability, I want the router to recognize those terms so exact signal phrasing is not required.
expected_behaviour_based_on_code: Catalog aliases, display names, IDs, and capabilities are normalized and scored as route matches.

user_story: As an agent, I want installed catalog skills detected from configured local roots so I know whether routing can proceed without installation.
expected_behaviour_based_on_code: detect returns each catalog entry with ready, partial, or missing status, installed path, available validation tools, and missing tools.

user_story: As an agent reviewing available Qlik sources, I want the catalog and local status summarized so I can explain available specialists.
expected_behaviour_based_on_code: list-sources returns catalog version, updated date, each entry ID, kind, display name, source repo, ref, local status, path, capabilities, tools, and routing signals.

user_story: As an agent planning an install, I want already-ready skills to report no required action so I avoid unnecessary writes.
expected_behaviour_based_on_code: install-plan for a ready skill returns action none, source details, current ready status, approval requirements, setup commands, and validation commands.

user_story: As an agent calling install-skill for a ready skill, I want an already-ready result so the output does not imply an install was planned.
expected_behaviour_based_on_code: install-skill for action none should return status already-ready and performed false.
pre_fix_result: Failed: returned status planned for an already-ready skill.
errors_documented: UX error: output implied a plan was made even though install_plan action was none.
fix_notes: install_skill now returns status already-ready and performed false when install_plan action is none.

user_story: As an agent missing a catalog skill, I want an install plan rather than an automatic install so approvals remain explicit.
expected_behaviour_based_on_code: install-plan for a missing skill returns action install, mode plan-only, source/ref/source_path, target path, setup/validation commands, and approval requirements.

user_story: As an agent invoking install-skill without execute, I want a plan-only response so no network, clone, or filesystem changes happen implicitly.
expected_behaviour_based_on_code: install-skill for a missing skill in plan-only mode returns status planned, performed false, and does not create the target directory.

user_story: As an agent given an undeclared skill ID, I want the install plan refused so prompt-supplied or unknown sources are not trusted.
expected_behaviour_based_on_code: install-plan for an ID not in the cached catalog returns action refuse with a reason that the skill is not declared.

user_story: As a CLI user checking an unknown skill, I want a structured not-in-catalog response and a successful command exit so the result can be parsed.
expected_behaviour_based_on_code: check-skill unknown emits JSON with status not-in-catalog and exits 0.

feature: Doctor validates registry, catalog, routes, and route tests
user_story: As a maintainer, I want doctor to validate the configured files and route expectations so changes fail fast before use.
expected_behaviour_based_on_code: doctor returns status ok with no errors when catalog schema, routing map references, and route tests all pass.

user_story: As a maintainer, I want incorrect route expectations reported clearly so routing regressions are visible.
expected_behaviour_based_on_code: doctor returns status failed and an error containing the expected and actual primary skill when a route test fails.

feature: Catalog sync updates cached snapshot after validation
user_story: As a maintainer refreshing public sources, I want sync-catalog to fetch, validate, and update the cached catalog when valid.
expected_behaviour_based_on_code: sync-catalog reads public_catalog.url, parses fetched JSON-compatible YAML, validates required catalog fields, writes cached_snapshot, and reports updated metadata.
pre_fix_result: Errored: PermissionError when Path.replace was blocked in the managed Windows workspace.
errors_documented: Logistical error: catalog sync depended on atomic replace being permitted even when normal file writes were allowed.
fix_notes: write_data_atomic now falls back to direct overwrite if atomic replace is denied, while keeping atomic replace as the normal path.

feature: Catalog sync rejects invalid fetched catalog
user_story: As a maintainer refreshing sources, I want invalid fetched catalog data rejected so a bad remote file does not replace local routing data.
expected_behaviour_based_on_code: sync-catalog returns failed with validation errors when fetched data is missing required catalog fields.

feature: Validation command handling for file checks
user_story: As an installer validating a skill, I want plain file validation commands to check existence without executing documentation files.
expected_behaviour_based_on_code: run_command returns returncode 0 and exists output when the command is an existing file with no arguments.

user_story: As an agent with approval to install a catalog skill, I want install-skill --execute to clone the approved source, copy the declared source path, skip repository/cache folders, and validate the installed skill.
expected_behaviour_based_on_code: install-skill with execute true uses the catalog install plan, runs git clone into a temporary clone root, copies source contents to the target without .git or __pycache__, runs setup and validation commands, and returns installed when all commands pass.
pre_fix_result: Passed in mocked non-network execution path.

user_story: As an agent invoking Qlik retrieval tooling, I want explicit qlik-ai-skill, qlik_knowledge_search, MCP server, or tool-first retrieval prompts to route to qlik-ai-skill while ordinary Qlik app and diagnostic prompts keep their existing specialists.
expected_behaviour_based_on_code: Explicit retrieval/tooling signals return primary_skill qlik-ai-skill with qlik-sense-app-dev fallback and qlik knowledge CLI/MCP tools; general app-development and diagnostic prompts still route to qlik-sense-app-dev or qlik-sense-diagnostic-tool.
pre_fix_result: Failed initially when a prompt containing qlik_knowledge_search and Set Analysis routed to qlik-sense-app-dev because Set Analysis scored higher.
errors_documented: Routing error: explicit retrieval tool invocation was not strong enough to beat overlapping Qlik topic terms.
fix_notes: Added explicit qlik_knowledge_search invocation phrases and MCP registration signals; added route and unit regression coverage.
post_fix_result: Passed: py -m unittest discover -s tests -v; py scripts\qlik_master.py doctor
